message:
refactor(service): rewrite ConcurrencyLimit as async state machine

The ConcurrencyLimit service's poll_ready() used try_acquire() with a
busy-loop fallback (wake_by_ref + return Pending), which caused spin
polling when no permits were available — wasting CPU cycles and starving
other tasks of scheduler time. The fix rewrites the permit acquisition
as a proper three-state machine:

- Idle: try synchronous try_acquire() first (fast path, zero overhead
  when permits are available)
- Acquiring: if sync fails, creates an OwnedAcquireFuture (the async
  semaphore acquisition path) and polls it, properly parking the task
  via the semaphore's internal waker registration
- Ready: permit acquired, ready for call()

This eliminates the busy-loop by leveraging the semaphore's native
async waker integration. The State enum also replaces the bare
Option<OwnedSemaphorePermit> field, making the state transitions
explicit and preventing misuse (call() panics with a clear message
if poll_ready() wasn't called).

Uses OwnedAcquireFuture (made public in the prior semaphore commit)
to name the future type in the State::Acquiring variant.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
